arts.ttl: Solskjaer Man Utd RETURN?, Chelsea's Osimhen MISTAKE, Arsenal transfer BLOW: FootballTransfers Recap
arts.sum: Solskjaer Man Utd RETURN?, Chelsea's Osimhen MISTAKE, Arsenal transfer BLOW: FootballTransfers Recap Solskjaer admits desire to replace Ten Hag as Man Utd manager

arts.ttl: 'Surprised': Jamie Redknapp changes his mind on Arsenal's summer deal for 'quality' player
arts.sum: Arsenal put Bolton to the sword and booked their ticket to the next round of the League Cup on Wednesday night at the Emirates. This...

arts.ttl: Arsenal's best players in 5-1 win over Bolton
arts.sum: A brace from young star Ethan Nwaneri and goals from Declan Rice, Raheem Sterling secured a very comfortable 5-1 win over the League One side, with the Gunners booking a fourth round clash with Preston North End at the end of October as a result. Nwaneri became the youngest player since Jack Wilshere way back into 2008 to score for the Gunners in the first half, sweeping home a Raheem Sterling cross at the back post.

arts.ttl: David Raya injury update: Progress and potential return date
arts.sum: It's understandable that Arsenal supporters are concerned over the Spaniard's current injury issue, with Raya now a doubt heading into the clash with newly-promoted Leicester City at the Emirates Stadium on Saturday. The specific nature of Raya's muscle injury is yet to be revealed, but Arteta is likely to provide an update on the goalkeeper before Saturday's Premier League clash.

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta makes exciting Ethan Nwaneri admission after Bolton brace
arts.sum: The Hale End graduate shared his'surreal' feelings after scoring two of Arsenal's five goals against Bolton. The 17-year-old helped his side cruise to victory at the Emirates in their midweek clash, making his second appearance for the first team this season.

arts.ttl: 'Showing his class': Paul Merson says £60,000-a-week player is finally shining after leaving Arsenal
Arsenal blasted for showing ‘inferiority complex’ against Man City as icon suggests title U-turn
arts.sum: “[Arsenal will] be coming away from that game thinking, ‘wow, they’re so much better than us, we’ll never win the league’,” Souness continued. The attitude of Mikel Arteta to play less adventurous was taken to the extreme against City and they could’ve gone about it in another way.”

arts.ttl: 'When I look': Paul Scholes tips Liverpool as bigger threat than Arsenal as he picks six players with 'wow factor'
arts.sum: Could Liverpool really win the Premier League?

arts.ttl: Steve Cooper rubbishes Arsenal theory in bid to claim first Leicester City win
arts.sum: Leicester are among six teams who have yet to register their first victory of the campaign but they have not lost in their last two league games - and they face Arsenal this weekend

arts.ttl: Arsenal set to suffer DOUBLE transfer blow
arts.sum: Arsenal could receive a double transfer blow with reports that two of their top targets are in talks over a new contract at their current clubs, ending Mikel Arteta’s chances of signing them. Two of Arsenal’s ideal targets this summer would have been Newcastle duo Anthony Gordon and Alexander Isak but new reports have claimed that both players could be in line for new contracts at St James’ Park.
